name: Land Surveyors
tagline: Find licensed land surveyors near you
description: The most comprehensive directory of licensed land surveyors across the United States — boundary, topographic, and ALTA surveys from vetted professionals.
certifications: NSPS (National Society of Professional Surveyors) member
cityPagePromptContext: Land surveyors are licensed professionals who measure and map property boundaries, perform topographic surveys, prepare ALTA/NSPS title surveys for real estate transactions, and provide expert testimony in boundary disputes. They work with homeowners, developers, attorneys, title companies, and municipalities. Common survey types include boundary, mortgage, ALTA/NSPS, topographic, construction staking, elevation certificate, and subdivision platting. Fees range from ~$500 for a simple residential boundary survey to $5,000+ for complex commercial or ALTA work. Every state licenses surveyors separately; each licensee signs and seals their deliverables.
buyerSearchContext: before a property transaction, dispute, or construction project

editorialAuthor.name: Nick Palmer
editorialAuthor.title: Founder & Lead Researcher
editorialAuthor.bio: Nick built this directory after a property-line dispute taught him just how much good surveyors matter — and how hard they are to find online.

schema.label: State Professional Land Surveyor License #
schema.scrape_hint: Look for 'License #', 'PLS #', or 'Registration #' in footer, about page, or contact section; often formatted as state abbreviation + digits (e.g., 'CA-12345')

schema.label: Licensed in States
schema.scrape_hint: Check service area descriptions, 'Licensed in' statements, footer disclaimers, or credential listings; may list multiple state PLS numbers or explicitly state multi-state coverage

schema.label: Years of Experience
schema.scrape_hint: Extract from 'About', 'Our Team', or principal surveyor bio mentioning 'X years experience', 'since YYYY', or 'established YYYY'; calculate from founding date if stated

schema.label: Services Offered
schema.scrape_hint: Scan 'Services', 'What We Do', or navigation menu for survey type keywords; look for service lists, project descriptions, or capability statements

schema.label: Technology & Equipment
schema.scrape_hint: Look in 'Technology', 'Equipment', 'Capabilities', or project case studies for mentions of GPS, drones, 3D scanning, robotic instruments, or software platforms (AutoCAD, Trimble, etc.)

schema.label: Certified Survey Technicians on Staff
schema.scrape_hint: Check team bios, credentials page, or 'About Us' for mentions of 'CST', 'Certified Survey Technician', or NSPS certification levels (CST I-IV)

schema.label: Residential Services
schema.scrape_hint: Identify from service lists, target market descriptions, or project galleries showing single-family homes, lot surveys, fence line disputes, or homeowner-focused language

schema.label: Commercial/Industrial Services
schema.scrape_hint: Look for mentions of commercial development, industrial sites, large-scale projects, ALTA surveys, or client lists including developers, architects, and engineering firms

schema.label: Typical Response Time
schema.scrape_hint: Extract from service guarantees, FAQ, or contact page stating 'respond within X hours/days', 'same-day quotes', or 'emergency availability'; convert days to hours if needed
